title: Why the second round of France's municipal elections matters
subhead: The vote on Sunday, March 22, will provide valuable national insights, with a focus on the far-right's result, the impact of mergers or divisions within the left and right and the right's gamble to stay in the race.

title: US and Iran trade threats of expanding war after strikes near sites tied to nuclear programs
Trump settles scores with former prosecutor Robert Mueller following news of his death
India faces major gas shortage as Middle East conflict disrupts supply
subhead: With restrictions, a black market and illegal storage networks, India's vulnerability has been exposed by the conflict affecting the Strait of Hormuz, through which 90% of its liquefied petroleum gas transits.

title: Cuba hit by second nationwide blackout in a week
subhead: Cuba's national power network failed for the second time in less than a week, leaving streets pitch black and forcing people to navigate using flashlights. Blackouts have intensified because of aging infrastructure and a US oil blockade.

title: Charif Majdalani: Lebanon is 'truly being dragged against its will into new calamities'
subhead: 'Writers on the War in the Middle East' 2/4. Le Monde asked writers Taleb Alrefai, Charif Majdalani and Lila Azam Zanganeh for their perspectives on the war.

title: Dnipro, the 'heart of Ukraine' where residents, refugees and military live side by side
subhead: This industrial city, considered a stronghold of the Jewish community, embodies the country's history and a certain idea of resistance to the advances of the Russian army, four years after the start of the war.

In Morocco's Agafay Desert, the Kasbah d'If combines luxury, serenity and vast open spaces
subhead: Located about 30 kilometers from Marrakech, the Kasbah d'If offers a stunning panorama of the Atlas Mountains. Built using adobe, this citadel-like 5-star hotel showcases Moroccan craftsmanship and a focus on well-being.

title: Michael Rider, Celine designer: 'Bringing joy and humor to fashion doesn't mean making funny clothes'
subhead: Since taking over as creative director of Celine in October 2024, the American designer, who succeeded Phoebe Philo and Hedi Slimane, has seized upon the brand's core principles, infusing them with his own positive and personal touch.

title: Despite exhaustion and fear of Iranian strikes, Israelis continue to support warring government
subhead: Nearly 80% of the population continues to support Benjamin Netanyahu's government after three weeks of conflict with Iran, fueled by the authorities' repeated announcements of victory.
